name: Thiamine (B1) sensor (TPP riboswitch + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects thiamine pyrophosphate (vitamin B1) in food and the gut using the natural TPP riboswitch — the most widespread riboswitch class — gating a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in probiotic Lactiplantibacillus plantarum.

input.detects: Thiamine/TPP in food, supplements, and the gut

sensing.sensorMechanism: The natural TPP riboswitch binds thiamine pyrophosphate and modulates downstream expression.
sensing.signalFlow: TPP/B1 -> TPP riboswitch gates an anti-reporter sgRNA -> CRISPRi inverts a constitutive reporter (NOT). Choose polarity for a turn-on-with-B1 readout.

parts.name: TPP riboswitch
parts.notes: Most widespread natural riboswitch; place upstream of the sgRNA.

output.result: Signal reports thiamine level (via the riboswitch/CRISPRi inversion).

performance.notes: The TPP riboswitch is the most widespread natural riboswitch; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: Auxotrophy and food-grade containment; GRAS probiotic host.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target/position the sgRNA; check L. plantarum WCFS1 off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Sensor -> sgRNA; dCas9; reporter. Use the host's standard toolkit.

buildSteps.title: Transform L. plantarum WCFS1
buildSteps.description: Transform, select, and confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample plus a thiamine standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the natural TPP riboswitch with L. plantarum CRISPRi
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
